texts.full_text: a local NGO with much experience working with street children. The name Ex-Cola (ex glue) denotes an institution working with children who sold glue to drug addicts. These girls and boys living on the streets also began to sell and consume other drugs such as marihuana and cocaine. The project started to work with girls after more and more girls began appearing on the streets. Girls tend to be more vulnerable to exploitation than boys. The aim of the project is, whenever possible, to get the girls and young mothers away from the streets and to reintegrate them in their own families and communities. To achieve this, values of spirituality, respect and non violence have to be established. the topics of the training courses were defined by the young mothers themselves; they include pregnancy, exploitation, drugs, infectious diseases, lack of protection, shelter and crime. The project works annually with a group of 20 young mothers and their children and even partners. With their intensive and integral approach each person of the group counts. There is not a percentage of success; all of them have to succeed. Even if one of the group appears in class under the influence of drugs, she will not be excluded. texts.full_text: iplex it accordingly. ```bash gst-launch-1.0 -e udpsrc multicast-iface="ethX" caps='application/x-rtp,media=(string)video,clock-rate=90000,payloads=%d' port 5004 ! \ rtspsrc location=http://localhost:8555/stream latency=-100000 name=rtps demuxer_mode=pull seek-mode=None do-timestamp=true stream-timeout-nsec=interval_presentation_time max-bytes-per-packet=max_packet_size_bytes ! mpegvideoparse queue ! avdec_h264 video/x-h265,profile=main,width=(int)1280,height(int)=720,fpsrange(30000/1001-60001)/2, sync=false async=true name=h26x sink_8b10bits ! ximagesink use-damage-region=no ``` ### Key Points: #### Sender: - `mpegtsmux`: This element is used to multiplex video and audio into MPEG-TS. You may need additional elements like `videotestsrc`, or an actual source for the stream. - The payload type (`pt=96`) must be consistent between sender, receiver pipeline configurations. #### Receiver: - `rtspsrc`: If you are using RTP over RTSP (common in streaming scenarios), use this element to handle incoming streams. Otherwise `udpcaps`. ### Troubleshooting Tips: 1. Ensure that your network configuration allows UDP traffic on the specified port. 2. Check if any firewall settings might be blocking these packets between sender and receiver machines. This setup should provide a basic framework for streaming webcam video using MPEG-TS with GStreamer, which you can further customize based upon specific requirements like audio streams or different codecs/containers as needed!

texts.full_text: dels of bladder cancer. The epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) is associated with aggressive phenotypes and is an independent predictor of stage progression and mortality in bladder cancer. Gefitinib ('Iressa,' ZD1839) is an orally active EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor. The objective of this study was to evaluate the in vitro and in vivo effects of gefitinib in the EGFR-expressing human bladder cancer cell lines 253J B-V, RT-112, and T24. EGFR expression was 3- and 2-fold higher in 253J B-v and RT-112, respectively, compared with T24 cells. Ten microm gefitinib inhibited EGFR, p42/44 extracellular signal-regulated kinase (ERK), and Akt/protein kinase B phosphorylation in all three of the cell lines. Inhibition of ERK by gefitinib was significantly greater in 253J B-V compared with RT-112 and T24 cells (9:2:1 in 253J B-V:RT-112:T24), wherethe differences were most pronounced. Gefitinib also induced a dose-dependent decrease in cell proliferation, which was more evident in the 253J B-V and RT-112 cell lines compared to T24. This effect correlated with the levels of EGFR expression and the extent of inhibition of downstream signaling pathways. In addition, gefitinib treatment led to a significant increase in apoptosis, particularly in the 253J B-V cells. In vivo studies using xenograft models of these cell lines showed that gefitinib administration resulted in a significant reduction in tumor growth, especially in tumors derived from 253J B-V and RT-112 cells. texts.full_text: public statements.” The McCourts split is a separation, with no word on whether a divorce is imminent. Neither has filed for divorce, according to Marshall Grossman, Frank’s attorney. You can read more about this story here, here and here. The two met back when they were students at Georgetown University and were wed in 1979. They have four sons. One son, Drew McCourt, serves as Director of Marketing for the club. Jamie McCourt is the CEO, and it is if she will remain in that position. Grossman went on to say: “Speculation about a potential sale of the team is rubbish. Frank McCourt is the sole owner. He has absolutely no intention of selling this team now or ever. This is not going to be another San Diego-like debacle.” He is referring to the divorce of John and Becky Moores, previous owners of the San Diego Padres, who later sold the team to Jeff Moorad. texts.full_text: unequal piston strokes. A dual opposed rocking piston pump with unequal strokes is also inherently out of balance. Because the strokes are different, the opposed reciprocating piston assembliesare traveling different distances during each revolution. As a result, the acceleration of one piston assembly is not equal to the acceleration of the other piston assembly. The diameters of the retainers, rod tops or diaphragms may or may notbe equal and the mass of the opposed reciprocating components may or may not be equal. As a result, the forces created by the opposed reciprocating components may not be equalresulting in unwanted shaking, vibration or noise.Co-pending U.S. application Ser. No. 11/776,310 addresses this problem, and is incorporated herein by reference. However, it would be desirable to reduce the size, weight and number of required parts for dual rocking piston pumps. texts.full_text: r sets of subkeys to be completely distinct from onanother, to the point of having different UIDs? In GnuPG, the concept of having distinct UIDs for subkeys isn't directly supported in the way you're describing. In the GPG (PGP) key structure, UIDs (User IDs) are tied to the primary key, not to subkeys. Each primary key can have multiple UIDs, but subkeys do not have their own UIDs. Instead, subkeys are tied to the primary key, and any certifications (signatures) are on the UIDs of the primary key, not on the subkeys themselves. However, you can achieve a similar effect through the following methods: 1. Using the Same Primary Key for Multiple Identities (with caveats): - You can add multiple UIDs to the primary key, each representing a different identity (e.g., work, personal). - You can then generate subkeys for signing and encryption, and control the use of these subkeys by specifying which subkeys to use for different contexts (e.g., using a specific subkey for signing work-related documents an

This is not supposed to be a pissing contest, and frankly, who wants to win this kind of contest. In any case, the numbers are a little fuzzy, but only around the edges. Nevertheless, they are good enough to show that around about the same proportions of New Zealanders and Australians were killed or wounded in World War 1, and at Gallipoli. My plea is this – that if you are commemorating Anzac Day in New Zealand, you do not forget the “A” (not that I have particularly noticed this to be the case back in the old country, but if you have different experiences, please let me know), and if you are commemorating Anzac Day in Australian, do not erase the “NZ.” They are equally important parts of the Anzac story.

This interdisciplinary talk features a panel of experts discussing how algorithms can perpetuate or challenge societal biases. The event is free for all to attend, with no tickets required. The panel includes: - Dr. Joy Buolamwini (MIT) - Dr. Pratik Agrawal (Stanford University) - Dr. Toni Williams (University of California, Berkeley) Dr. Joy Buolamwini will give a keynote talk titled "Place and Displacement: Bias in Our Algorithms and Society." She is the founder of the Fairness, Accountability, Transparency (FAccT) Lab at MIT, where she focuses on developing tools for understanding and mitigating bias. Dr. Pratik Agrawal will present a talk titled "The Ethics of Algorithmic Governance: From AI to Human Rights." He is an associate professor in the Department of Computer Science at Stanford University, where he researches algorithms that can help solve social problems. Dr. Toni Williams' presentation title and topic are not yet available. The event will be moderated by Dr. Mireille E. Rosay (UC Berkeley), who leads CDSS's work on algorithmic fairness in data science education. Biography Azevedo was born in São Luís, to DavidAluísio Azevedo was born on April 14, 1857, in São Luís, Maranhão, Brazil. His father was David Gonçalves de Azevedo, a noted writer and journalist who had a significant influence on his son's literary development. Aluísio began his career as a caricaturist for several newspapers in Rio de Janeiro, where he moved with his family when he was still a child. He also worked as a playwright, writing comedies that were well-received by the public. In 1880, he published his first novel, "Uma Lágrima de Mulher," which marked the beginning of his literary career. The following year, Azevedo made a significant contribution to Brazilian literature with the publication of "O Mulato." This novel introduced Naturalism to Brazil, a literary movement that sought to portray human behavior and social conditions realistically, often focusing on the darker aspects of life. 'O Mulato' addressed issues of racism and social inequality, themes that would become central to his writing. Azevedo's adherence to the Naturalist movement brought him both acclaim and controversy. His novels were praised for their realistic portrayal of Brazilian society but also criticized for their explicit content and challenging of social norms. Despite the controversies, he continued to write prolifically, producing novels, short stories, and plays that explored the human condition within

texts.full_text: on December 23, 2010 So, it’s the holidays and I travel a lot around the holidays. Recently, I sat next to a woman on a short hour-long flight. We did the little awkward half-smile when she sat down, then I promptly fell asleep before the plane took off. About half way through the flight, I awoke and somehow got chatting with my neighbor. Only a few sentences in, “it” came up. She’d asked a simple question, but the answer would disclose that I have narcolepsy. Should I divert the conversation or tell her? I decided to go for it. I told her I have narcolepsy and knew exactly what was coming next — she paused for a split-second and nodded her head “yes,” in apparent recognition of narcolepsy. I used to cringe at the pause-and-nod, fearing where the convo would go from there, as it could take one of many directions. The person could ask, “So could you fall asleep right this second?” or they might offer an anecdote about a time they fell asleep in an odd place. I used to smile and nod back politely, realizing we weren’t on the same page. Worst of all, the pause-and-nod coud be followed by something like: “I think it may runin my family.” That one still makes me laugh because usually that person doesn'tsleep during day time or wake up uncontrollably in movies at inappropriate times and is not diagnosed with narcolepsy. texts.full_text: between therapeutic and supratherapeutic ranges would be a valuable diagnostic tool, particularly in delirious and unconscious patients. 108 patients treated with amitriptyline, 8 patients treated with doxepin, 10 patients treated with clozapine, and 72 normal control subjects matched for age and sex were tested for heart rate variability while resting. Considering time and frequency derived measures, which are rather independent of heart rate, the patients showed significantly decreased heart rate variability parameters (p < 0.0001), as compared with the normal subjects. Of the patients presenting delirious symptoms 6 showed coefficientsof variation in R-R intervals below a certain threshold that could reliably indicate supratherapeutic levels of tricyclic antidepressants, while none were observed among those within therapeutic ranges or control groups. This finding suggests decreased heart rate variability (HRV) as an effective supplementary screening method for identifying potential overdosage with these medications. In clinical practice where traditional assessment methods may be challenging due to altered mental status in patients presenting delirious symptoms—often seen during intoxication—it could serve a critical role, especially when rapid decision-making is required. Further research should focus on establishing specific HRV cutoff values and exploring the sensitivity of this method across different tricyclic antidepressants. Additionally, it would be beneficial to assess its applicability in diverse patient populations beyond those studied here—such as individuals with pre-existing cardiac conditions or concurrent medication use—to ensure broader clinical utility. In conclusion, decreased R-R interval variation presents a promising criterion for identifying overdosage of amitriptyline and potentially other tricyclic antidepressants. texts.full_text: Why The Continental Drift-Pangaea And Tiny Expanding Earth Theories are Wrong. Hello…It is okay to take the pictures of the continents from a round globe and put them on a surface. We call that a map. Then when looking at the map it is obvious that some continents were connected. Many more pictures telling the rest of the story on my other pages. Just click from the # menu above. . When wanting to know whether all the continents were connected and how, some folks go a step further. For an some take the map pictureand, on a flat map, the continents appear to fit together, leading to the idea that all the continents were once connected. However, the flat map picture, while visually appealing, does not accurately represent the Earth's true shape and the true distances and relationships between continents. The Earth's shape, while not perfectly flat, does not perfectly fit the shape of the flat map. The true distances and relationships between continents, while not perfectly flat, are not accurately represented on the flat map. texts.full_text: A: If you don't want to edit the Ant script, you can add -keep options to proguard.cfg forthe specific classes or packages within the `.jar` files that you want to preserve during the ProGuard processing. However, if you want to exclude entire `.jar` files from being processed by ProGuard at all, you need to adjust your ProGuard configuration file (`proguard.cfg`) accordingly. Here's how you can do it: 1. Identify the JAR Files: First, identify which `.jar` files you want to exclude from obfuscation. 2. Exclude JAR Files: - You can use `-libraryjars` and `-dontwarn` options in your ProGuard configuration file to specify that certain libraries should not be processed. - Alternatively, if you want to ensure that specific classes or packages within a `.jar` are kept intact, you would use the `-keep` option. Here is an example of how you might configure ProGuard to exclude a specific JAR file: ```proguard # Keep all classes in a specific jar from being obfuscated -keep class com.example. { *; } -libraryjars /path/to/your/excluded.jar # If you want to prevent warnings about the excluded library -dontwarn com.example. ``` If you want to exclude multiple JAR files, you can list them all in the `-libraryjars` directive: ```proguard -libraryjars /path/to/jar1.jar -libraryjars /path/to/jar2.jar ``` However, if your goal is to keep specific classes or packages within a `.jar` file from being obfuscated, you would use the `-keep` option like this: ```proguard # Keep all classes in a package from being obfuscated -keep class com.example. { *; } # Keep specific classes from being obfuscated -keep public class com.example.MyClass { *; } ``` Make sure to replace `com.example` and `MyClass` with the actual
